name: Capitol Punishment Chili
recipeIngredient: 2 kg extra lean chuck (ground for chili)
1 kg extra lean pork (ground for chili)
500 g extra lean chuck (cut into 5-mm cubes)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large pot, combine paprika, oregano, MSG, chili powder, cumin, beef bouillon, beer, and 2 cups of water. Let simmer.
2. In a separate skillet, brown the meat with oil or kidney suet until lightly browned.
3. Drain the meat and add it to the simmering spices.
4. Continue adding meat until all is incorporated.
5. Saute finely chopped onions and garlic in oil or kidney suet.
6. Add to the spice and meat mixture. Add water as needed. Simmer for 2 hours.
7. Add mole powder, sugar, coriander, hot pepper sauce, and tomato sauce. Simmer for an additional 45 minutes.
8. Dissolve masa harina in warm water and stir into the chili. Add salt to taste. Simmer for another 30 minutes.
This recipe comes from Bill Pfeiffer, who won the 1980 and 1992 World Chili Cookoff and took first place in the International Chili Society's 14th annual World Championship Chili Cookoff with it. He calls this recipe "Capitol Punishment Chili" because he won his first attempt at a chili cook-off in Washington, DC. Texas red chili has no beans.
I use Durkee's Louisiana Red Hot sauce. Any favorite brand of beer will do; Pfeiffer used Budweiser as the sponsored contest beverage. For spicier chili, add additional Louisiana Red Hot sauce to taste. This chili is best enjoyed with plenty of time for simmering.
